cooperates.cooperate_desc: Hanabi works well with Lolita because Lolita blocks projectile damage and absorbs burst, allowing Hanabi to keep her shield active and stay crowd-control immune while dealing sustained damage.
Hanabi works well with Floryn because global healing and shields help her stay alive, maintain her shield for crowd-control immunity, and survive burst during fights.
Hanabi works well with Diggie because his time-freezing ultimate and crowd-control immunity allow her to safely deal damage and maintain shields during team fights.
Hanabi pairs with Estes because his constant healing keeps her shield up longer, maintaining her crowd-control immunity and allowing sustained damage in fights.

counters.counter_desc: Alucard counters Hanabi because he can instantly dive into melee range with lifesteal and burst, bypassing her shield-based crowd-control immunity and lack of mobility.
Saber counters Hanabi because his instant lock-on burst and airborne suppression can kill her before she can maintain shields or react, exploiting her lack of mobility.
Julian counters Hanabi because his magic damage and control skills can quickly break her shields, reducing her passive CC immunity and making her vulnerable.
Gloo counters Hanabi because his sticky and burst damage can lock her down and reduce her mobility, making it hard for her to maintain shields or stay safe.

dominants.dominant_desc: Always think that early game is only for building your items, stay safe and be careful for potential ganks of enemy team. I recommend to last hit the last minion on the wave to get enough gold, always check if you can keep up with your enemies, but if not. Just playsafe and priority to build your items like trinity build first.

This is the game changing point, mid game is important to know who's team is gonna lead the game, in this point. I can recommend you to go with your team for teamfights, always stay at the back if you think enemy can take you down easily but if not you should not worry. You should push lane more often in mid game since it can help your team to pressure enemies and take objectives. Just take advantage of mid game and you'll be good.

This is the peak of the game, lategame is the point where enemy is also full build, but with great team composition and communication, you can win this. for Hanabi, you should always check for opportunity on when you'll go aggressive on teamfights, always think that you're the most important in lategame since Hanabi is the one who can clear lanes fast. Avoid ganks and always go with your tank. Take the lord if possible and stay back. Then when you take lord and going towards enemy base, you have two options. Burst enemies with your ult and skills since they're focus on lord, or you can towerlock their base, depends in situation.

emblems.emblem_desc: Hanabi has a lot of emblem set, this one is for casual playing Hanabi and for also competitive rank. Swift for extra 10% attack speed at the start of the game, self explanatory. Next is Bargain hunter, this is for early item for Hanabi and having 95% of their base price can help Hanabi to build faster. The last one is Quantum charge for extra damage for basic attack and also movement speed, this also restores HP which is good for Hanabi.

This is for Hanabi CD Reduc set, this help Hanabi specially to the skilled players to use her skills much often, this is for spamming her skills having low cool down and also for her spell Aegis, reducing it's cool down to 75% to 60%, this help much having ready active spell to counter ganks, And Quantum charge, Sefr explanatory.

This is Hanabi Snowball emblem set, the one I'm currently using now and the one I can recommend to the Hanabi pro players. with this Hanabi can deal good amount of damage in early game, it benefits her bouncing attack more on this because of the damage can do. Swift is self explanatory, Weapon master talent is for Hanabi increase damage to snowball in early game. Weapon master can help a lot for overall Hanabi performance, The last one is war cry. I know War cry don't count Hanabi multi hit attacks to activate the war cry and hitting enemy only counts as one, still Hanabi separate hits can still activate the war cry. making her damage increase more with the 8% of war cry.

Moving to the fighter emblem new passive, Dealing hybrid lifesteal is useful for both Fighter and marksman heroes now. This emblem set provide sustainability during team fights specially early game. First of all is swift for extra attack speed since we using fighter emblem, it will lack attack speed provided by marksman emblem itself. Swift makes fighter emblem useful for Hanabi, Next is weapon master for more damage etc, and Weakness finder for long team fights, making Hanabi stand much longer and finish the game.

equips.equip_desc: This is for Hanabi most used build in latest season, also the build recommend if you're using Hanabi. Perfect for using when enemy doesn't have much for burst damage dealer. Still this build works well because of the malefic gun and rose gold meteor, providing long range and survivability shield.

This CD Reduc works well if enemy marksman is weak against Hanabi, this build can sustain Hanabi and have more survivability because of low CD of skills that you can use more often, this also benefits Hanabi ult to have lower cool down specially lategame to control enemies. Now maybe you're asking why there's hunter strike? Well ist because if you already build Hanabi trinity build, Hanabi now can deal good amount of damage and attack speed, so having hunger strike benefits her in this build. Hunter strike gives 15+ pen and also 10+ CD Reduc that helps her skills, not only that but hunter strike passive also works to Hanabi, hitting enemy and having 50% extra moveset speed that decays overtime. Rose gold for survivability.

This is for Hanabi Snowball build that I'm currently using, this build provides good amount of damage specially to early game and Snowball enemies. As usual build we change Abit. First we have the rapid boots, we need that for extra movement speed and Regen effect so we can heal enough in teamfights. Next we build sky Peircer, yes I know you're thinking that Hanabi with  sky Peircer is a bit risky since of her vulnerability and need to stack. Hanabi Snowball build core is Sky piercer, we need that to easily pick off enemy in early game, it also benefits Hanabi passive by bouncing attacks, it can take down enemies and kill instantly. This can also burst enemy in lategame, providing massive amount of damage. If you build this, you just need to be careful in early because it might lack of damage but if you snowball with this, you can now be aggressive with your teammates. I do not recommend this if your team is constantly getting killed, you can stay with normal build to have enough Damage. Now for wind talker, this works with the attack speed and movement speed, this benefits alot with sky Peircer. After you build all of these, you can sell rapid boots for any survivability/defense item, only if you needed

This build for fighter emblem is the same as the build we use to  go with Hanabi, but with little changes. You can choose either warrior and tough boots, it's for sustainability since enemy can't easily take you down early with Hanabi first skill immunity and aegis. Next is malefic gun, if you don't need range base on enemy composition, you can go with malefic roar instead for more pen but if not, stick with malefic gun for distance. This build set will work very effective on fighter Hanabi, you'll notice the difference in team fights. Using this build and the recommended emblem set, with player skills. Hanabi can be meta and beat the meta marksman now like Claude, Karrie, etc.

hero.hero_overview:
Hanabi is the one of marksman hero that is rarely used in Mlbb, specially high ranks because of the changing of meta. Hanabi is also good for casual and competitive play because it's easy to master her skills. Hanabi is also Considered as "Queen of dark system" because of Hanabi is used by low ranks and having skilless players that push her for being the dark system, but that doesn't mean Hanabi is useless, Using Hanabi is still on the players experience and skill and better performance. I'm Kous3ii the one of top global Hanabi user, Making this guide for  other Hanabi mains there to help them improve using her and to master her, this guide intends to make you to make better of using Hanabi.


[Background]
As the oldest daughter of the Scarlet Supreme Grandmaster, Hanabi had been used to being the best amongst her peers. It was after the arrival of Hayabusa, a young ninja of the Shadow Sect, that Hanabi realized she was met with a strong rival... Through the rivalry that lasted their entire adolescence, Hanabi slowly fell in love with her rival—Hayabusa. But when she finally worked up the courage to reveal her feelings. Hayabusa had left without saying goodbye...
hero.hero_strength: Let's start off by Hanabi strength, Hanabi is Damage/finisher hero who's strong specially mid to lategame due to her sustain auto attacks and damage output, Hanabi is also have passve that can convert her lifesteal into shield that grants her immunity and also crowd control effect we call Cc immune. Hanabi is also multi Target hero that deals damage with her enhance basic attacks that helps her to clear the lanes much faster and deal good amount of damage making pressure to Hanabi specially lategame.
hero.hero_weakness: As for Hanabi weakness, Hanabi is also have bad side having no mobility. She lacks of mobility because of her skillset, having no built in dash or blink skill that makes her vulnerable against assassin's and ganks, making her an easy target and easy to kill hero in early game. Hanabi si weak in early game Without having her first or even third item but as the mach progress, Hanabi is also going to be useful at mid and lategame.

data.recommend: Keep Hanabi at the backline, protected by supports, and use Aegis or shields to maximize her passive immunity during team fights. Use these guide for your own prefer play style.

spell.spell_desc:
For me the best spell for Hanabi is aegis because it can help Hanabi stay longer on teamfights, having immunity, sustainability and also the shield with this spell. She can clear enemy faster and pick off one. Another reason for this is because Hanabi have no mobility skill like dash, this can help Hanabi more than using flicker spell.


As I said, Why flicker is not good for Hanabi? Well the reason for that is Hanabi is weak in early game, yes flicker can help for potential ganks but Hanabi vulnerability is still problem. That's why i prefer aegis for Hanabi spell.

data.title: TOP GLOBAL HANABI GUIDE BY: Kous3ii

hero.hero_overview: Hanabi is a Marksman in Mobile Legends known for her powerful AoE (Area of Effect) damage and late-game scaling. She excels in team fights with her bouncing auto-attacks and immunity to crowd control, making her a formidable force when fully farmed.
hero.hero_strength:
✔ High AoE damage with bouncing attacks.
✔ Late-game powerhouse with strong scaling.
✔ Immune to CC when shielded.
hero.hero_weakness:
✖ Weak early game and farm-dependent.
✖ Low mobility, making her vulnerable to assassins.
✖ Requires proper positioning to maximize her damage.

spell.spell_desc:
Aegis (Battle Spell)

Aegis grants a shield that absorbs 750 (+50 per level) damage for 3 seconds. If there are nearby allied heroes, the shield is also applied to the nearest ally with the lowest HP (but at 50% effectiveness).

Cooldown: 90 seconds

Best Used For:

Protecting yourself or an ally during fights.

Countering burst damage from assassins and mages.

Enhancing survivability for low-mobility heroes like Mark

data.title: Broken damage

dominants.dominant_desc: At lvl 1 you i prefer upgrade the 2nd skill first to create the minion. You can keep the safe distance from enemy, to chase you can rely on "Quantum charge" and to escape if the timing war right, since 'quantum charge' have 8s cool down, further more you can escape combining her 1st skill. Make sure skills 2 and 3 hit the opponent to gain more damage.
dominants.dominant_title: Before level 4, during laning phase and war

dominants.dominant_desc: Hanabi already have enough damage and sustainability if she already has Windtalker and Haas' Claws, but reaching her highest peak damage after build the Barserkes's Fury. For Wind of Nature and Rose Gold Meteor is conditional. This build is gaining bigger and more "burst" damage rather than "Corrosion & Golden Staff" build giving faster time to execute the enemy, and of course giving bigger impact on team fight.

emblems.emblem_desc: This 'Fatal' talent allows Hanabi gain massive critical damage even without extra attack speed from her 1st skill. 'Weapon master' to synergy with 'fatal' to cover the overall damage. 'Quantum charge" as I was saying this critical build doesn't always depends on her first skill to enhance her damage, I wanted to keep her 1st skill to escape, so to chase enemies 'Quantum charge' is a must.

equips.equip_desc:
This critical build allowing Hanabi to kill the enemy faster without her 1st skill, while combining marksman critical emblem could still boost her damage and life steal. Yes this build offering better damage and life steal but her shield collected from her 1st skill would not that thick, yet again better life steal and damage. Why is this better than Corrosion & Golden staff build? The life steal it still there even without her 1st skill in this critical build

More over to explain:
Windtalker: Boosting her damage and clear lane faster, since it's gain bouncy damage to the creep and enemy
Swift boots: actually it's really depends on the enemy compositions, but swift boots more preferable to boost her attacks speed after buying windtalker
Haas' claws: it's great for Hanabi since her basic attack is bouncy she can gain more life steal than any other hero, while boosting her critical chance and attack speed.
Berserker's Furry: this item to finish her critical build, gain massive critical attack and critical chance, 
Wind of Nature: it's to sustain from enemy assassin, it's conditional you can build this item any slot you want, either it's 2nd or 3rd
Malefic Roar: to penetrate enemy tank

Explanation:

Berserker's Furry: this item to finish her critical build, gain massive critical attack and critical chance, 
Wind of Nature: it's to sustain from enemy assassin, it's conditional you can build this item any slot you want, either it's 2nd or 3rd
Rose Gold Meteor: tis item is not a core item for Hanabi in this build, but it's perfect to sustain from enemy mage, combining with her 1st skill, it work just perfect

hero.hero_strength: She is stronger if she can can bounce her skill, especially if there's 2 target, the first target will take up to 1.5 damage. Could possibly avoid being ganked, and avoid offset with her 1st skill,
hero.hero_weakness: The damage is not that big if the target only 1. While she can avoid being ganked and offset, she's still have a big chance of not surviving, since she just walk,

data.recommend: It's for user Hanabi who is bored with attack speed build, meanwhile this critical build actually giving more damage and sustainability for Hanabi,

spell.spell_desc: In this critical attack build Hanabi basic attack already good without the extra attack speed gained from her 1st skill, so you can keep her 1st skill to avoid offset. And battle spell flicker would be perfect for extra 'run'

item.title: Kalea

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Most tank that has CC works great with Hanabi. One that has stun, root, and displacements to keep them away from her to give her space to damage enemy team. And even if they somehow reach Hanabi. The shields of her build makes root, stuns, and taunt null against her. So yeah any tanks os great for her.

Squishy supports are a no go for Hanabi. Has enough ability to heal (lifesteal) and shield(1st skill, bloodwings, etc) to keep herself in top condition. Examples of these are Diggie, Floryn, Estes, Rafaela,etc.

counters.counter_desc: Really strong against mid range enemies like Moskov thanks to Hanabi's passive. Make sure to not trade auto attacks with Moskov and use your passive and 2nd skill to your advantage. Other champs that I think are in the same situation are Karrie, Claude, Miya, etc

dominants.dominant_desc: Early levels hanabi is strong against most MM heros with our emblems and AA's bouncing effect. BUT take note. Its important to not exchange blows. Use Hanabi's bouncing AA's and 2nd skill to your advantage. Follow the sequence of item I made. Strive to end the game before late game start (like everyone is at Level 13-15) Once late game happens, if you have low stacks in your SP, change it to BOD. Beside that probably know how to buy and sell for Revive, and the frozen item.
dominants.dominant_title: Guides for this Build as a Mythic Hanabi Enjoyer

emblems.emblem_desc: Normal crit MM emblem. The weapon master is a must as a 5% increase on the item's shielding ability is important

equips.equip_desc: First, haa's claws for the crit and lifesteal. With how we pick the emblems crit is our main source of damage here. Next is Corrosion scythe as Hanabi is the type of hero that benefits with this item's effect greatly. Tough boots for overall tenacity and compensation for the lack of magic resist. Sky Piercer as this build is mostly for clean ups and chasing enemy to death and keeping distance while kiting. Blood Wings the huge reason why I made this guide.

hero.hero_overview: Hanabi
hero.hero_strength: Play if there's mostly squishy enemy and assassins too. Would be a beast if you know how to kite properly using this build. Strong against heros that stun to initiate you like saber, tigreal, atlas, cyclops, etc.
hero.hero_weakness: Weak against those that can burst you in one hit.
